laws.actName: State Emblem of India (Regulation of Use) Rules, 2007
laws.title: Restriction on the use of emblem on business cards, letter heads, etc.
laws.description: No person (including former functionaries of the Government of India or the State Governments) shall use the emblem on their business cards or letter heads. [cite: 31, 32]
laws.simpleExplanation: You are not allowed to print the State Emblem on your personal or business visiting cards or letterheads, even if you previously held a government position. [cite: 31, 32]

punishments.description: As per Section 7 of the parent Act (2005), contravention of use restrictions carries imprisonment up to two years or fine up to five thousand rupees, or both. [cite: 106]

relatedSections.description: Restricts the use of the emblem on souvenirs, trophies, and other items. [cite: 35]

laws.actName: State Emblem of India (Regulation of Use) Rules, 2007
laws.title: Restriction on the use of emblem on souvenirs, trophies, etc.
laws.description: No person shall use the emblem on any souvenir, trophy, memento, or badge etc. [cite: 35]
laws.simpleExplanation: The National Emblem cannot be placed on awards, trophies, or badges, except for those specifically issued by the Government or for specific official sporting events as permitted. [cite: 35, 36]

punishments.description: Punishable under the parent Act for improper use. [cite: 106]

relatedSections.description: Permits the emblem on trophies/mementos given by the Government or for State-recognized bodies for sporting events. [cite: 36]
